title: The Experiences of Loveday Brooke_ Lady Detective
description: The Experiences of Loveday Brooke_ Lady Detective — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: Accomplice
memes.description: The role of the accomplice — the person who aids or enables a crime without being its principal perpetrator — in the Loveday Brooke detective stories.

memes.label: Alexander Henderson
memes.description: Alexander Henderson, known as 'old Sandy,' the lodge-keeper at Troyte's Hill whose murder is investigated by Loveday Brooke.

memes.label: Alguida
memes.description: A small hamlet in South Italy, home to Count Mascagni and his daughter Lena, whose disappearance and supposed death drive the plot of "Missing!"

memes.label: Arrest
memes.description: The legal procedure of arrest in the Loveday Brooke detective stories, where suspects are taken into custody by police or detective authority.

relations.label: The Black Bag Left on a Doorstep

memes.label: Arrest of Harry Emmett
memes.description: The arrest of Harry Emmett, footman and reciter, on a warrant for complicity in the Craigen Court robbery, concluding Loveday Brooke's investigation in "The Black Bag Left on a Doorstep."

memes.label: Brighton
memes.description: The south-coast resort town that serves as a key location in "The Ghost of Fountain Lane" and appears as a waypoint in "The Redhill Sisterhood," embodying the intersection of holiday leisure, religious diversity, and criminal activity in late-Victorian England.

memes.label: Carriage
memes.description: Horse-drawn and railway carriages feature prominently across the Loveday Brooke stories as settings for key encounters, undercover work, and travel between investigations.

memes.label: "Chapter 1: The Black Bag Left on a Doorstep"
memes.description: "A Christmas Eve jewellery robbery at Lady Cathrow's country house"

memes.label: "Chapter 2: The Murder at Troyte's Hill"
memes.description: "A suspicious death investigated at Troyte's Hill in Newcastle"

memes.label: "Chapter 3: The Redhill Sisterhood"
memes.description: "A mysterious convent-like sisterhood under investigation"

memes.label: "Chapter 4: A Princess's Vengeance"
memes.description: "A tale of royal intrigue and revenge"

memes.label: "Chapter 5: Drawn Daggers"
memes.description: "A case involving a threatening letter and drawn daggers"

memes.label: "Chapter 6: The Ghost of Fountain Lane"
memes.description: "A mysterious ghostly apparition on Fountain Lane"

memes.label: "Chapter 7: Missing!"
memes.description: "A missing person case that tests Loveday Brooke's skills"

memes.label: Cheque
memes.description: A stolen cheque drawn on the West Sussex Bank, central to the fraud investigation in "The Ghost of Fountain Lane."

memes.label: Cipher
memes.description: The use of cipher and coded communication by the Lynch Court detective agency, a distinctive element of Loveday Brooke's professional practice.

memes.label: Clue
memes.description: Clues as the fundamental unit of detective investigation in the Loveday Brooke stories — the fragments of evidence, observation, and testimony that Loveday pieces together to solve cases.

relations.label: The Black Bag Left on a Doorstep

memes.label: Concept Index
memes.description: Thematic index of all concepts in the Loveday Brooke detective stories, covering investigative methodology, legal concepts, and social themes.

memes.label: Confession
memes.description: The confession — a suspect's admission of guilt — as a dramatic and narrative device in the Loveday Brooke detective stories, where confessions often reveal more than they intend.

memes.label: Coroner
memes.description: The coroner — the official who presides over inquests into violent or unexplained deaths — as a recurring institutional presence in the Loveday Brooke detective stories.

relations.label: The Black Bag Left on a Doorstep

memes.label: Count Mascagni
memes.description: An Italian count of Alguida, South Italy. The biological father of Irené Golding; his secret first marriage to Irené's mother is the key to unraveling the mystery of Irené's disappearance in the seventh story.

memes.label: Craigen Court
memes.description: The country residence of Sir George Cathrow, where the theft of Lady Cathrow's jewels — the central crime of the first Loveday Brooke story — takes place.

memes.label: Craigen Court Safe
memes.description: The safe in Lady Cathrow's dressing-room at Craigen Court from which £30,000 worth of jewellery was stolen in The Black Bag Left on a Doorstep (ch01).

relations.label: The Black Bag Left on a Doorstep

memes.label: Cumberland
memes.description: A historic county in the north of England, location of the Troyte's Hill estate and home to the Craven family in "The Murder at Troyte's Hill."

memes.label: Detective Methodology
memes.description: The systematic investigative methods employed by Loveday Brooke and the Lynch Court detective agency, combining careful observation, logical reasoning, and practical fieldcraft in late-Victorian criminal investigation.

relations.label: *The Experiences of Loveday Brooke, Lady Detective*

memes.label: Disappearance of Irené Golding
memes.description: The disappearance of Irené Golding, only daughter of Richard Golding of Langford Hall, investigated by Loveday Brooke in "Missing!"

memes.label: Disappearance of Lucie Cunier
memes.description: The disappearance of Lucie Cunier, amanuensis to Mrs. Druce, investigated by Loveday Brooke in "A Princess's Vengeance."

memes.label: Disguise
memes.description: The use of disguise and assumed identities as a detective technique employed by Loveday Brooke in her investigations.

memes.label: Drawn Daggers Incident
memes.description: The sending of threatening dagger drawings through the post to the Rev. Anthony Hawke, connected to the theft of Miss Monroe's diamond necklace in "Drawn Daggers."

relations.label: the theft of Miss Monroe's diamond necklace

memes.label: "Dyer's Detective Agency"
memes.description: "Dyer's Detective Agency, located in Lynch Court off Fleet Street, is the private investigation firm run by Ebenezer Dyer that employs Loveday Brooke as its principal lady detective."

memes.label: Ebenezer Dyer
memes.description: Ebenezer Dyer, chief of the well-known detective agency in Lynch Court, Fleet Street, and Loveday Brooke's employer.

memes.label: Event Index
memes.description: Chronological and categorical index of all events in the Loveday Brooke detective stories.

memes.label: Evidence
memes.description: The gathering, analysis, and presentation of evidence in criminal investigation, as practiced by Loveday Brooke and the Lynch Court detective agency.

memes.label: False Accusation
memes.description: The danger of false accusation — the wrongful imputation of guilt to an innocent person — as a recurring concern in the Loveday Brooke detective stories, where official suspicion frequently falls on the wrong target.

memes.label: Fleet Street
memes.description: A historic street in the City of London, off which Lynch Court — the location of Dyer's Detective Agency — is situated, anchoring the Loveday Brooke stories in the professional heart of Victorian London.

memes.label: Fountain Lane
memes.description: A small turning in Brighton where a supposed ghost haunting terrorizes the inhabitants, forming the central mystery of "The Ghost of Fountain Lane."

memes.label: Fraud
memes.description: The crime of fraud — financial deception and confidence tricks — as depicted in the Loveday Brooke detective stories, particularly in cases involving impersonation and forged documents.

memes.label: Hafiz Cassimi
memes.description: Son of a Turkish-Egyptian diplomatic attaché. A romantic rival to Princess Dullah-Veih in the fourth story; disliked by several characters who consider him untrustworthy.

memes.label: Harem Mission
memes.description: The Harem Mission, a philanthropic society in A Princess's Vengeance (ch04), established on lines similar to the Zenana Mission with Princess Dullah-Veih as its distinguished president.

memes.label: Harry Emmett
memes.description: Harry Emmett, the handsome young footman involved as an accomplice in the Craigen Court jewel robbery, whose arrest Loveday Brooke orchestrates.

memes.label: Inquest
memes.description: The coroner's inquest as a formal legal proceeding in Victorian England, a recurring element in the Loveday Brooke detective stories.

memes.label: Inspector Griffiths
memes.description: Inspector Griffiths of Scotland Yard, who works alongside Loveday Brooke on several cases and represents the official police approach to criminal investigation.

memes.label: Inspector Ramsay
memes.description: Inspector Ramsay, a "lanky, bony Scotchman, sandy-haired and slow of speech" — the local police inspector at Langford Cross who enlists Loveday Brooke to investigate the disappearance of Irené Golding.

memes.label: Investigation
memes.description: The methods and practice of criminal investigation, both private and official, as depicted in the Loveday Brooke detective stories.

memes.label: Irené Golding
memes.description: The only daughter of wealthy Australian merchant Richard Golding, whose disappearance from Langford Hall drives the case in "Missing!"

memes.label: Lady Cathrow's Jewellery
memes.description: The collection of jewellery worth £30,000 stolen from Lady Cathrow at Craigen Court in The Black Bag Left on a Doorstep (ch01), the theft that initiates Loveday Brooke's first recorded case.

relations.label: The Black Bag Left on a Doorstep

memes.label: John Murray
memes.description: The landlord of 7 and 8 Paved Court, Redhill, whose report to the police triggered the investigation of the Redhill Sisterhood.

memes.label: John Rogers
memes.description: A former elder of the Wesleyan chapel in Gordon Street, Brighton. Suspected of stealing a cheque and fleeing; his disappearance complicated the investigation into the Fountain Lane ghost hoax.

memes.label: Jury
memes.description: The jury — the body of citizens who deliver verdicts at inquests and trials — as a recurring element in the legal framework of the Loveday Brooke detective stories.

relations.label: The Black Bag Left on a Doorstep

memes.label: "Lady Cathrow"
memes.description: "Lady Cathrow is a character in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 'The Black Bag Left on a Door-Step' (chapter 001 of *The Experiences of Loveday Brooke, Lady Detective*). The victim of a £30,000 jewellery theft, she is the wife of Sir George Cathrow and the employer of Stephanie Delcroix."

relations.label: *The Experiences of Loveday Brooke, Lady Detective*

memes.label: Lady Gwynne
memes.description: Lady Gwynne, a society lady and intimate friend of Mrs. Druce, who brought Lucie Cunier from a Geneva orphanage to England. A key figure in the conspiracy to remove Lucie from Major Druce's attentions.

memes.label: Langford Cross
memes.description: The railway station serving Langford Hall and the surrounding Leicestershire district.

memes.label: Langford Hall
memes.description: The country estate of Richard Golding in Leicestershire, where Irené Golding went missing.

memes.label: London
memes.description: The primary metropolitan setting of the Loveday Brooke detective stories, where the detective agency is based and where much of the action originates.

memes.label: Lord Guilleroy
memes.description: Lord Guilleroy, a wealthy landowner who owns nearly all the land for miles around Langford, and devoted suitor of Irené Golding. A man of strong common sense and energy, instrumental in the investigation of Miss Golding's disappearance.

memes.label: Loveday Brooke
memes.description: Loveday Brooke, the protagonist lady detective of the seven stories, employed by Ebenezer Dyer's detective agency in Lynch Court, Fleet Street.

memes.label: Lucie Cunier
memes.description: The young Frenchwoman who served as amanuensis to Mrs. Druce and whose disappearance drives the case in "A Princess's Vengeance."

memes.label: Lynch Court
memes.description: The office location of Ebenezer Dyer's detective agency on Fleet Street, London, serving as the operational headquarters throughout all Loveday Brooke stories.

memes.label: Magistrate
memes.description: The magistrate — a justice of the peace who issues warrants and conducts preliminary hearings — as a recurring figure in the legal machinery of the Loveday Brooke detective stories.

relations.label: The Black Bag Left on a Doorstep

memes.label: "Major Druce"
memes.description: "Major Druce is a character in Catherine Louisa Pirkis's 'A Princess's Vengeance' (chapter 004 of *The Experiences of Loveday Brooke, Lady Detective*). Son of Mrs. Druce, engaged to Princess Dullah-Veira, he becomes a key figure in Loveday Brooke's investigation into a series of strange occurrences."

relations.label: *The Experiences of Loveday Brooke, Lady Detective*

memes.label: Maria Lisle
memes.description: A former servant whose gaunt figure haunted the garden of a Brighton cottage during the Fountain Lane ghost investigation.

memes.label: The Marquise Ring
memes.description: The marquise ring worn by Irené Golding on the third finger of her left hand, whose disappearance from her body is the crucial clue in Missing! (ch07).

memes.label: Martha Watts
memes.description: A young servant girl in Brighton who was the first witness to the so-called "ghost" of Fountain Lane. Her testimony, dismissed by local police as imagination, proved to be a crucial element in Loveday Brooke's unraveling of the ghost hoax.

memes.label: Master Harry
memes.description: The young son of Mr. Craven of Troyte's Hill, whose mysterious illness is central to the murder investigation in "The Murder at Troyte's Hill."

memes.label: Merstham
memes.description: A village and railway station in Surrey, situated just before Redhill on the London–Brighton line, used by Loveday Brooke as a discreet arrival point during her investigation.

memes.label: Métropole Hotel
memes.description: A grand hotel on the Brighton seafront where Loveday Brooke takes a room to recover from overwork, and which becomes the base for her investigation in "The Ghost of Fountain Lane."

memes.label: Miss Craven
memes.description: Miss Craven, the delicate daughter of Mrs. Craven, who assists her mother in the elaborate scheme to help her brother Harry escape after the murder of Sandy the lodge-keeper at Troyte's Hill.

memes.label: Miss Monroe
memes.description: An heiress from Peking, daughter of Sir George Monroe, sent to England under the guardianship of the Reverend Anthony Hawke to escape an undesirable suitor. Central figure in the diamond necklace mystery of "Drawn Daggers."

memes.label: Miss Monroe's Necklace
memes.description: The diamond necklace lost by Miss Monroe in Drawn Daggers (ch05), a theft that Loveday Brooke investigates at the request of Sir George Cathrow.

memes.label: The Missing Photograph
memes.description: The photograph of Lucie Cunier that goes missing in A Princess's Vengeance (ch04), a piece of evidence whose disappearance points to the involvement of Hafiz Cassimi in the young woman's disappearance.

memes.label: Motive
memes.description: The psychological drives and purposes behind criminal acts in the Loveday Brooke detective stories, where motive is treated not as an investigative starting point but as a conclusion reached through the systematic assembly of evidence.

relations.label: *The Experiences of Loveday Brooke, Lady Detective*

memes.label: Mr. Clampe
memes.description: Mr. Clampe, an innkeeper from Brighton who brings a cheque fraud case to Loveday Brooke's attention and serves as her driver and local guide to East Downes in "The Ghost of Fountain Lane."

memes.label: Mr. Craven
memes.description: The elderly owner of Troyte's Hill in Cumberland, whose concealed insanity led him to murder his lodge-keeper and whose guilt Loveday Brooke alone was able to expose.

memes.label: Mr. Golding
memes.description: Richard Golding, a wealthy Australian merchant and father of Irené Golding. Resident of Langford Hall, Leicestershire, whose daughter's disappearance drives the mystery of "Missing!"

memes.label: Mr. Hawke
memes.description: The Reverend Anthony Hawke, a clergyman of the Church of England who acts as guardian to Miss Monroe. Client who brings the diamond necklace mystery and the "drawn daggers" threats to Dyer's Agency.

memes.label: Mrs. Brown
memes.description: Mrs. Brown, a stout, rosy-cheeked village woman and mother of the schoolmistress in "The Ghost of Fountain Lane." Loveday Brooke's landlady during her investigation of the mysterious Maria Lisle.

memes.label: Mrs. Craven
memes.description: Mrs. Craven, wife of Mr. Craven of Troyte's Hill, mother of Harry and Miss Craven. A "tall, sharp-featured woman" with missionary experience in Natal, who orchestrates the elaborate cover-up of her son's murder of Sandy the lodge-keeper.

memes.label: Mrs. Druce
memes.description: Mrs. Druce, mother of Major Druce, a wealthy society hostess and philanthropist. A well-known figure in London society, promoter of the Harem Mission and employer of Lucie Cunier, whose disappearance drives the plot of "A Princess's Vengeance."

memes.label: Mrs. Greenhow
memes.description: Mrs. Greenhow, a widow acting as housekeeper to Richard Golding and chaperon to his daughter Irené at Langford Hall. Engaged to marry Mr. Golding, her relationship with Irené is notably strained.

memes.label: Mrs. Turner
memes.description: Mrs. Turner, the young and fashionably-dressed wife of the Reverend Charles Turner, Vicar of East Downes. Suspected of stealing and cashing a blank cheque for £600 during her husband's absence.

memes.label: Mrs. Williams
memes.description: Mrs. Williams, the housekeeper at Craigen Court, a "worthy and discreet" widow who provides Loveday Brooke with cover identity as her visiting niece "Jane Smith" during the investigation of the Cathrow jewellery robbery.

memes.label: Murder
memes.description: The crime of murder as depicted in the Loveday Brooke detective stories, including the Troyte's Hill case and other suspicious deaths investigated by Loveday.

memes.label: Newspapers in the Stories
memes.description: The role of newspapers and press accounts as sources of information, narrative framing, and investigative material across the Loveday Brooke detective stories.

relations.label: The Black Bag Left on a Doorstep

memes.label: North Cape
memes.description: A country house near Redhill, Surrey, notable for its electric lighting installation — one of the earliest in the district.

memes.label: Observation
memes.description: The detective's faculty of close observation — a core skill exemplified by Loveday Brooke throughout the stories.

memes.label: Organization Index
memes.description: Complete index of all organizations in the Loveday Brooke stories — detective agencies, police forces, missions, banks, and other institutions.

memes.label: Paris
memes.description: The French capital, appearing prominently in "A Princess's Vengeance" and "Missing," serving as a destination for flight, a hub of international police cooperation, and a symbol of the cosmopolitan world beyond England's shores.

memes.label: Paved Court
memes.description: A blind alley off the London and Brighton coach road near Redhill, housing the suspected headquarters of the Redhill Sisterhood at numbers 7 and 8.

memes.label: Person Index
memes.description: Complete alphabetical index of all person entries in the Loveday Brooke wiki.

memes.label: Place Index
memes.description: Complete geographical index of all locations in the Loveday Brooke detective stories.

memes.label: Portland Place
memes.description: A broad street in Marylebone, London, approximately twenty minutes' drive from Lynch Court, visited by Loveday Brooke during the investigation in "A Princess's Vengeance."

memes.label: Portmanteau
memes.description: The black leather portmanteau left on a doorstep, containing stolen jewellery, that initiates the first Loveday Brooke investigation.

memes.label: Princess Dullah-Veih
memes.description: A Turkish-Egyptian princess involved in the disappearance of Lucie Cunier in "A Princess's Vengeance."

memes.label: Profession (Detective)
memes.description: The professional identity of the detective — particularly the female detective — as explored in the Loveday Brooke stories, including Loveday's entry into the profession and her relationship with her male colleagues.

memes.label: Redhill
memes.description: A town in Surrey, within the Reigate police district, serving as the primary setting for "The Redhill Sisterhood" investigation.

memes.label: Redhill Sisterhood Burglaries
memes.description: A series of burglaries in the Redhill district linked to a newly established undenominational Sisterhood, investigated by Loveday Brooke.

memes.label: Reigate
memes.description: A town in Surrey, seat of the Reigate police jurisdiction under Inspector Gunning, referenced in multiple Loveday Brooke stories as the local police authority for the Redhill district.

memes.label: Respectability
memes.description: The Victorian social value of respectability — the appearance of good character and social standing — as a recurring theme in the Loveday Brooke detective stories, where it serves both as a shield for the guilty and a vulnerability for the innocent.

memes.label: Reward Handbill (Irené Golding)
memes.description: The "Five Hundred Pounds Reward" handbill offering a reward for information about the missing Irené Golding, which introduces the final Loveday Brooke story Missing! (ch07).

memes.label: Richard Golding
memes.description: Father of Irené Golding, owner of Langford Hall in Langford Cross. An English gentleman struggling to maintain a depleted estate; his daughter's disappearance forms the central mystery of the seventh story.

memes.label: Robbery
memes.description: The crime of robbery as a central plot element in the Loveday Brooke stories, particularly the country-house jewel theft that opens the collection.

memes.label: "Scotland Yard"
memes.description: "Scotland Yard is the headquarters of the Metropolitan Police in London, appearing as a recurring institution throughout *The Experiences of Loveday Brooke, Lady Detective*. It serves as both a resource and a foil to Loveday Brooke's independent detective work, with constables and detectives from Scotland Yard assisting in several cases."

relations.label: *The Experiences of Loveday Brooke, Lady Detective*

memes.label: Sir George Cathrow
memes.description: Sir George Cathrow, baronet and owner of Craigen Court, victim of a £30,000 jewellery robbery on Christmas Eve. Known for his punning wit and generous hospitality.

memes.label: Sir George Monroe
memes.description: The father of Miss Monroe, a diplomat who sent his daughter a valuable dagger from Peking in "Drawn Daggers."

memes.label: Sister Anna
memes.description: A young, sweet-faced Sister at the undenominational Redhill Sisterhood. Described as having a "sweet, sad face" and tending to crippled children; Loveday Brooke took a particular interest in her welfare.

memes.label: Sister Monica
memes.description: A senior member of the undenominational Sisterhood at Redhill who brought the burglary case to Dyer's detective agency. Described as a tall, coarse-featured woman with a hard, unpleasant voice, a "masculine-looking" woman.

memes.label: Stephanie Delcroix
memes.description: Stephanie Delcroix, the young French lady's maid at Craigen Court who falls under suspicion after the jewel robbery and whose innocence Loveday Brooke establishes.

memes.label: Suspicion
memes.description: The role of suspicion as the starting point of criminal investigation in the Loveday Brooke stories.

memes.label: "Telegrams in the Stories"
memes.description: "Telegrams serve as a recurring narrative device throughout *The Experiences of Loveday Brooke, Lady Detective*. These urgent messages propel plot developments across multiple stories, particularly in 'Missing!' (chapter 007), where a telegram from Paris sets off a chain of critical events."

relations.label: *The Experiences of Loveday Brooke, Lady Detective*

memes.label: "Telegrams in the Stories"
memes.description: "Telegrams are a recurring documentary form across the Loveday Brooke stories, serving as communication tools, narrative devices, and vehicles for the Lynch Court agency's cipher system."

relations.label: *The Experiences of Loveday Brooke, Lady Detective*

memes.label: "The Black Bag"
memes.description: "The black bag is the central physical object in 'The Black Bag Left on a Door-Step' (chapter 001). Found on Lady Cathrow's doorstep, it contains seemingly insignificant contents that become crucial evidence in Loveday Brooke's investigation."

relations.label: The Black Bag Left on a Door-Step

memes.label: The Black Bag Robbery
memes.description: The jewel robbery at Craigen Court on Christmas Eve, in which Lady Cathrow lost £30,000 worth of jewellery, investigated and solved by Loveday Brooke.

memes.label: "The Drawn Daggers"
memes.description: "The drawn daggers are a series of threatening sketches sent through the post in 'Drawn Daggers' (chapter 005). These ink drawings serve as the central physical evidence in Loveday Brooke's analysis of the case."

memes.label: "The Experiences of Loveday Brooke, Lady Detective"
memes.description: "*The Experiences of Loveday Brooke, Lady Detective* is a collection of seven detective short stories by Catherine Louisa Pirkis, first published in 1894. Featuring one of the earliest female detectives in English fiction, the stories follow Loveday Brooke as she solves cases across Victorian England and beyond."

relations.label: The Experiences of Loveday Brooke, Lady Detective

memes.label: "The Experiences of Loveday Brooke, Lady Detective"
memes.description: "The Experiences of Loveday Brooke, Lady Detective (1894) is a collection of seven detective short stories by Catherine Louisa Pirkis, featuring one of the earliest female detectives in English fiction. The stories were originally serialized in The Ludgate Monthly (1893–1894) before publication as a collected volume."

memes.label: The Ghost of Fountain Lane
memes.description: The mysterious appearance of a ghost in Fountain Lane, Brighton, investigated by Loveday Brooke and revealed as a hoax connected to a stolen cheque in "The Ghost of Fountain Lane."

memes.label: The Murder at Troyte's Hill
memes.description: The murder of Alexander "Sandy" Henderson, lodge-keeper at Troyte's Hill, by his employer Mr. Craven — a crime born of thirty years of blackmail and deepening insanity, solved by Loveday Brooke.

memes.label: The Reciter's Treasury
memes.description: The Reciter's Treasury, a cheap shilling volume that Loveday Brooke uses as a prop in her undercover investigation in The Black Bag Left on a Doorstep (ch01).

relations.label: The Black Bag Left on a Doorstep

memes.label: Redhill Sisterhood
memes.description: An undenominational Sisterhood at Redhill, suspected of coordinating country-house burglaries under the guise of charitable work.

memes.label: The Stolen Cheque
memes.description: The theft and fraudulent presentation of a cheque belonging to the Rev. Charles Clampe, connected to the ghost hoax in Fountain Lane, Brighton.

memes.label: Theft of Miss Monroe's Necklace
memes.description: The theft of Miss Monroe's valuable diamond necklace at the home of the Rev. Anthony Hawke, investigated by Loveday Brooke in "Drawn Daggers."

memes.label: Three Bridges
memes.description: A railway junction station on the London–Brighton line where Loveday Brooke and Inspector Gunning change trains during the Redhill investigation.

memes.label: Troyte's Hill
memes.description: A country estate in Cumberland, owned by Mr. Craven, where the murder of the lodge-keeper forms the central mystery of "The Murder at Troyte's Hill."

memes.label: Verdict
memes.description: The legal verdict — the formal finding of a coroner's jury or trial jury — as a recurring narrative element in the Loveday Brooke detective stories.

memes.label: Warrant
memes.description: The arrest warrant as a legal instrument in the Loveday Brooke detective stories, issued by a magistrate and served by police to authorize the detention of suspects.

relations.label: The Black Bag Left on a Doorstep

memes.label: Wesleyan Chapel, Gordon Street
memes.description: The Wesleyan chapel in Gordon Street, Brighton, where Richard Steele served as minister and around which the conspirators in The Ghost of Fountain Lane (ch06) organized their fraudulent scheme.

memes.label: West Sussex Bank
memes.description: The West Sussex Bank, the financial institution in The Ghost of Fountain Lane (ch06) on which the stolen cheque at the centre of Loveday Brooke's investigation was drawn.

memes.label: Witness
memes.description: The role of the witness — the person who gives testimony based on direct observation — in the criminal investigations of the Loveday Brooke stories.

memes.label: Wootton Hall
memes.description: A country house near Redhill, Surrey, with complicated passages, splendid family plate, and recently rebuilt.

memes.label: Zenana Mission
memes.description: The Zenana Mission — an older, traditional missionary society focused on women's welfare in India — referenced in the Loveday Brooke story "A Princess's Vengeance" as the model upon which the fictional Harem Mission was based.
